groups.name: alkali metal
groups.description: Soft, highly reactive metals in Group 1 that readily lose their single valence electron. They have low melting points and react vigorously with water to form alkaline hydroxides.

groups.name: alkaline earth metal
groups.description: Reactive metals in Group 2 with two valence electrons. They are harder and denser than alkali metals, with higher melting points, and form oxides that are basic (alkaline).

groups.name: transition metal
groups.description: Metals in Groups 3–12 with partially filled d-orbitals. They exhibit multiple oxidation states, form colored compounds, and are generally hard, dense, and good conductors.

groups.name: post-transition metal
groups.description: Metals located between transition metals and metalloids, with higher electronegativities than typical metals. They tend to be softer, with lower melting points, and show more covalent bonding character.

groups.name: lanthanide
groups.description: The 15 elements from lanthanum to lutetium (57–71) with partially filled 4f orbitals. They are silvery metals with similar chemical properties, used in magnets, lasers, and electronics.

groups.name: actinide
groups.description: The 15 elements from actinium to lawrencium (89–103) with partially filled 5f orbitals. All are radioactive; uranium and plutonium are used in nuclear energy and weapons.

groups.name: metalloid
groups.description: Elements with properties intermediate between metals and nonmetals, found along the staircase line on the periodic table. They are semiconductors, making them essential for electronics.

groups.name: diatomic nonmetal
groups.description: Nonmetals that naturally exist as diatomic molecules (H₂, N₂, O₂, F₂, Cl₂, Br₂, I₂). They are generally highly electronegative and essential to biological and chemical processes.

groups.name: polyatomic nonmetal
groups.description: Nonmetals that form molecules or network structures with more than two atoms, such as carbon, sulfur, selenium, and phosphorus. They exhibit diverse allotropic forms.

groups.name: noble gas
groups.description: Extremely stable, unreactive gases in Group 18 with full valence electron shells. They have very low boiling points and are used in lighting, welding, and cryogenics.

groups.name: unknown
groups.description: Recently synthesized or hypothetical elements whose chemical properties have not been confirmed experimentally due to their extreme instability and short half-lives.
